schema.name: cgt-figure-manifest
schema.description: Machine-readable manifest for the checksum-pinned CGT website figure release.

interpretation: minor_polish is the release curation state; it does not mean that every publisher-specific typography check passed.

Figures 1–3 are core main candidates; Figure 4 is synthesis/supporting material suitable for main or supplementary placement; Figure 5 is supporting material suitable for supplementary placement or a compact main panel.

recovery_capture.purpose: Authoritative file-level hashes used for local integrity verification.

qa_summary.figure_02: Minimum text size 4.35 pt is below the 5–7 pt target.
qa_summary.figure_03: Minimum text size 4.20 pt is below the 5–7 pt target.
qa_summary.figure_04: Minimum text size 4.50 pt is below target; three clipping diagnostics were recorded.
qa_summary.figure_05: A text audit exists, but no Nature-compliance report was found.

exclusions.reason: Exploratory prototype material, not part of the frozen canonical figure set.

caveats: This manifest records the selected release and its provenance; it does not itself establish peer review, reproducibility outside the captured environment, or causal validity.
All figures remain in the freeze state minor_polish, with publisher-specific issues documented in qa_summary and per-figure QA.
The recovery bundle verifies capture integrity but is candidate/unreviewed and explicitly does not establish a CORD result.
CGT topography assets were excluded because the freeze report classifies them as exploratory.
Scientific interpretation must preserve the claim boundaries recorded in the associated legends, methods, source data, notebooks, and claim ledger.

figures.label: Figure 1
figures.title: Residual perturbation geometry predicts external CRISPR-derived gene fitness
caveats: The fitness analysis is retrospective and gene-level; it does not establish causal direction.
Reported model coefficients are conditional on the fitted specification and should not be interpreted as universal biological effects.

figures.label: Figure 2
figures.title: Residual perturbation geometry is recurrent across datasets and contexts

qa.source_note: minimum text 4.35 pt fails 5–7 pt target

caveats: Recurrence within sampled datasets and contexts does not by itself demonstrate transportability to unseen perturbation regimes.
More stringent exclusion tests attenuate generalization and define an important boundary on the claim.

figures.label: Figure 3
figures.title: Signed dimensions of residual perturbation geometry map to candidate biological axes

qa.source_note: minimum text 4.20 pt fails 5–7 pt target

caveats: Axis labels are candidate biological interpretations, not definitive mechanism assignments.
Over-representation, rank-based support, predictive coefficients, and essentiality correlations are partly dependent evidence streams and are not causal tests.

figures.label: Figure 4
figures.title: Integrated evidence atlas and hierarchical model of CGT

qa.source_note: minimum text 4.50 pt fails; 3 clipping diagnostic rows

caveats: The atlas synthesizes evidence used elsewhere in the project and therefore is not an independent validation dataset.
The hierarchical constraint model is a supported interpretive hypothesis, not evidence of causal universality.

figures.label: Figure 5
figures.title: TCGA tumors projected into independently defined CGT axis space

qa.source_note: no Nature-compliance report; text audit exists

caveats: Bulk-tumor projections can reflect lineage, purity, stromal composition, and microenvironmental signal in addition to tumor-intrinsic state.
Residualization can remove biologically meaningful variation; the projection is orthogonal support rather than causal validation.

figures.label: Supplementary Figure 1
figures.title: Current generalization boundary of the CGT fitness predictor
caveats: This sensitivity figure bounds the fitness result and should be interpreted with Figure 1.
Performance under harder exclusions is attenuated and should not be generalized beyond the evaluated holdout designs.

figures.label: Supplementary Figure 2
figures.title: Repeated-label residual reliability depends on context breadth

qa.source_note: minimum text 4.35 pt fails 5–7 pt target

caveats: Reliability is conditional on repeated labels and the contexts represented in the available data.
Within-context agreement does not establish cross-context transportability.

figures.label: Supplementary Figure 3
figures.title: External pathway support for six supporting CGT axes

qa.source_note: minimum text 4.20 pt fails 5–7 pt target

caveats: Pathway support is associative and helps interpret axes; it does not identify a unique mechanism.
The displayed evidence streams are not statistically independent.

figures.label: Supplementary Figure 4
figures.title: Full collapsed-axis evidence atlas and curation outcome

qa.source_note: minimum text 4.50 pt fails; 3 clipping diagnostic rows

caveats: The full atlas is a synthesis and curation record, not an independent replication.
Axis consolidation and labels reflect analytical choices documented by the associated notebook, legend, and methods artifacts.

figures.label: Supplementary Figure 5
figures.title: TCGA expression quality-control summary

qa.source_note: no Nature-compliance report; text audit exists

caveats: This panel documents expression-data quality control and is not a biological validation result.
It inherits the bulk-tumor and residualization limitations of Figure 5.

figures.label: Supplementary Figure 6
figures.title: TCGA principal-component variance spectrum

qa.source_note: no Nature-compliance report; text audit exists

caveats: The variance spectrum is a dimensionality diagnostic rather than evidence for a specific biological mechanism.
It inherits the bulk-tumor and residualization limitations of Figure 5.
